attacking_style: Opportunistic bridging rider who strikes from late breakaways (1km to go) or exploits chaos in reduced sprint finishes rather than pure acceleration. Thrives in hard semi-classics stages with 8,000+ ft climbing that eliminate pure sprinters.
team_tactics: Receives exceptional team support including GC leaders (Vingegaard, Roglič) sacrificing positioning for leadouts in final kilometer. Also deployed as high-tempo domestique and defensive closer of dangerous moves within 1-2km.
terrain_preference: Hard semi-classics stages combining significant climbing (9,000ft+) with uphill or technical finishes where pure sprinters are dropped. Excels on 2km puncheur finishes and cobbled uphill sections. Vulnerable on any time trial distance.
vulnerability: Time trials at any distance—lost yellow jersey by 30s on short climbing TT. Crashes repeatedly in critical moments (wet descents, finale positioning). Lacks final 100m explosive acceleration against pure sprinters when positioned perfectly.
key_patterns: Receives extraordinary team support: GC leaders Vingegaard/Roglič reverse hierarchy to leadout in finals (4 observations)
Crashes in critical race phases: wet descents, finale positioning, selection moments expose positioning risks (4 observations)
Wins from opportunistic late moves not pure sprint: bridging at 1km, catching solos in final meters (3 observations)
Strong on climbing-heavy stages eliminating pure sprinters, then delivering finishing speed (4 observations)
Time trial weakness consistent: lost yellow by 30s on short TT, finished 21st at 1:50 (2 observations)
Dual role flexibility: wins stages opportunistically while executing extended domestique work same race (5 observations)
form_trajectory: neutral: 2023 showed stage wins and yellow at Dauphiné but also crashes and confirmed TT weakness. Role remains hybrid domestique/opportunist rather than clear leadership trajectory.
built_from: 2022 Ronde van Vlaanderen - Tour des Flandres
